mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in nonfarm payrolls signals expanding labor income and aggregate demand, which drives higher corporate revenues and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC of PAYEMS changes versus SPX 20-day returns falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An elevated federal funds rate above two and a half percent tightens financial conditions, raises borrowing costs, and compresses equity valuations, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next sixty trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are positive in more than 55 percent of instances when FEDFUNDS is above 2.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales growth coinciding with stable consumer prices signals robust real demand without inflationary margin compression, jointly producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: The joint z-score of RSAFS and CPIAUCSL fails to produce Rank IC above 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate during late-cycle expansions signals deteriorating labor markets and falling household income, triggering corporate earnings downgrades and negative S&P 500 returns over the next sixty trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises more than 0.2 percentage points over prior 3 months
failure_condition: During late-cycle months, UNRATE increases coincide with positive S&P 500 60-day returns in more than 50 percent of triggered observations.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month decline in industrial production signals weakening manufacturing output and supply-side contraction, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next five trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day S&P 500 returns are nonnegative in greater than 50 percent of months when INDPRO drop exceeds 1.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: A significant drop in housing starts combined with a restrictive federal funds rate signals a housing-led recession driven by tight credit conditions, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next sixty trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls > 10 percent over 3 months while FEDFUNDS > 4 percent
failure_condition: Joint signal occurrences do not produce negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in at least 60 percent of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real gross domestic product signals expanding aggregate economic output and corporate earnings capacity, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDPC1 growth rate changes show Rank IC below 0.02 against 20-day SPX returns over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation exceeding five percent annualized signals an overheating economy that prompts tighter monetary policy expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next five trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 5-day returns are positive in more than half of the months when CPIAUCSL year-over-year change is above 5.0 percent.
